/cwe/weakness/459.Name: Incomplete Cleanup
/cwe/weakness/459.Description: The product does not properly "clean up" and remove temporary or supporting resources after they have been used.

CommonConsequences.Note: It is possible to overflow the number of temporary files because directories typically have limits on the number of files allowed. This could create a denial of service problem.

Entries.IntroText: Stream resources in a Java application should be released in a finally block, otherwise an exception thrown before the call to close() would result in an unreleased I/O resource. In the example below, the close() method is called in the try block (incorrect).

PotentialMitigations.Description: Temporary files and other supporting resources should be deleted/released immediately after they are no longer needed.
